name: D-CAS – Distributed Content-Addressable Storage
raw_facts: Language: Go 1.23 (uses standard 'net', 'os', and 'sync' for core networking and storage).
Architecture: Built a custom P2P transport layer over TCP with a thread-safe peer management system using sync.RWMutex.
Content-Addressing: Implemented file storage indexed by SHA-256 checksums, ensuring data deduplication and integrity (Content-Addressable Storage).
Networking: Developed a decoupled 'TCPTransport' with a callback-based 'HandshakeFunc' for extensible peer validation.
Concurrency: Utilized a non-blocking accept loop with dedicated goroutines for handling individual peer connections (P2P).
CLI & Tooling: Integrated a command-line interface with a Makefile-based workflow for orchestrated node deployment and testing.
Testing: Leveraged 'github.com/stretchr/testify' for unit testing storage handlers and network protocol logic.
business_impact: Eliminated data redundancy across distributed clusters through hash-based content addressing.
Established a horizontally scalable storage foundation where nodes can be added dynamically without downtime.
